untranslatable_reason: This is an options spread strategy (calendar spread: short April 50 call / long July 50 call). The signal describes a multi-leg derivative position with specific expiration dates and net debit calculations. Standard equity price-action indicators cannot express options spreads, implied volatility decay, calendar spread dynamics, or the mechanics of selling near-term and buying longer-term options. The entry condition is simultaneous execution of two option legs, not a price/technical condition on the underlying.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_87 is a calendar spread strategy requiring options chain data and expiration management. Entry requires: (1) Sell April 50 call, (2) Buy July 50 call simultaneously at net debit of 3 points. Exit conditions stated as AUTHOR_SILENT; a reasonable reversal would be close at strike or expiration. This strategy cannot be accurately backtested on equity indicators alone—it requires an options-aware engine with Greeks, time decay, and volatility surface modeling.
